skill.name: lay-press-release-writer
skill.description: Transform complex academic papers into accessible university press releases for general audiences, alumni, and media.
skill.summary: Convert academic papers into engaging lay press releases for university newsrooms.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 61 patterns across 5 files (872 lines), including misidentified 'weak cryptographic algorithm' and 'Ruby backtick execution' findings. All 61 static findings were evaluated as false positives caused by the analyzer misclassifying Chinese characters, markdown code formatting backticks, and ordinary English words (e.g., 'Transform', 'Discover') as security threats. The only real security consideration is a user-controlled file output path at scripts/main.py:290, which is within the skill's declared scope of writing press release output. No prompt injection, credential theft, data exfiltration, or malicious code was found. The skill performs exactly what it claims: converting academic papers into university press releases.

low_findings.title: Unvalidated file output path
low_findings.description: The script accepts a user-controlled --output parameter for file writing without path validation or restriction to a workspace directory. While within the skill's declared scope for writing press release output, the path is not validated against traversal or restricted locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-controlled file write via command-line argument
dangerous_patterns.description: The --output argument allows specifying an arbitrary file path for JSON output. The open() call at line 290 writes content to this user-controlled path without validation. This is a declared capability but could allow writing outside intended directories in non-sandboxed environments.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static analyzer false positive: markdown backticks misidentified as shell execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged 35 markdown inline code backticks and code blocks in SKILL.md and references/audit-reference.md as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution.' These are all markdown formatting used for documenting commands, not actual runtime backtick execution. The legitimately documented commands (python, pip) are standard Python tooling expected for a Python-based skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static analyzer false positive: text misidentified as weak cryptographic algorithm
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged 21 instances of 'weak cryptographic algorithm' across SKILL.md, scripts/main.py, and lay-press-release-writer_audit_result_v2.json. These are all ordinary text strings (e.g., 'Transform', 'Discover', 'Research', Chinese characters) that coincidentally matched a cryptographic pattern signature. No cryptographic code exists in this skill.

content.user_title: Turn Academic Papers Into Engaging Press Releases
content.value_statement: Academic papers use complex language that general audiences cannot understand. This skill transforms research papers into clear, university-style press releases that maintain scientific accuracy while making findings accessible to the public, alumni, and media.
actual_capabilities: Extracts key findings and innovation points from academic papers using regex-based pattern matching
Generates university press center style press releases with headlines, subheadlines, and datelines
Produces templated researcher quotes and institutional boilerplate text for Chinese universities
Simplifies academic language into accessible terminology using a built-in replacement dictionary
Structures output in standard inverted pyramid press release format with lead, body, and boilerplate
Supports multiple target audiences including general readers, alumni, and media with adjustable tone
limitations: Requires full paper text or a detailed abstract as input to produce meaningful output
Generated researcher quotes are templated placeholders and not from actual interviews
Institution boilerplate only covers a limited set of Chinese universities with templates
Does not perform fact-checking or verify the scientific claims made in input papers

use_cases.title: University Communications Office
use_cases.description: Press officers transform faculty research papers into newsroom-ready press releases without hiring external writers or spending hours simplifying complex research.
use_cases.target_user: University press officers and communications staff

use_cases.title: Research Lab Public Outreach
use_cases.description: Lab managers and principal investigators convert published papers into accessible summaries for stakeholder reports, funding agency updates, and department newsletters.
use_cases.target_user: Research lab managers and principal investigators

use_cases.title: Journal Editor Lay Summaries
use_cases.description: Academic journal editors create lay summaries of accepted papers to promote research findings to broader audiences, journalists, and social media channels.
use_cases.target_user: Academic journal editors and publication staff

prompt_templates.title: Basic Paper to Press Release
prompt_templates.prompt:
Convert this academic paper into a university press release:

[Paste full paper text or abstract here]
prompt_templates.scenario: You have a paper and need a quick press release draft without additional customization or metadata.

prompt_templates.title: Press Release with Institution Details
prompt_templates.prompt:
Create a press release from this paper with full attribution:

Paper text: [Paste paper content]
Paper title: [Paper title]
Institution: [University name]
Authors: [Author names, comma-separated]
Publication venue: [Journal or conference name]
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a press release with proper institution attribution and author credits for official university communications.

prompt_templates.title: Audience-Targeted Press Release
prompt_templates.prompt:
Generate a press release tailored to a specific audience from this research:

Paper text: [Paste paper content]
Institution: [University name]
Publication venue: [Journal name]
Target audience: [general, alumni, or media]
Tone: [formal, friendly, or inspiring]
prompt_templates.scenario: You need different versions of the same press release adapted for different audience groups such as alumni magazines or media outlets.

prompt_templates.title: Full Script Execution with All Parameters
prompt_templates.prompt:
Run the complete press release generation with the packaged script:

python scripts/main.py --paper-text "[Full paper text or abstract]" --paper-title "[Paper title]" --authors "[Author1,Author2,Author3]" --institution "[University name]" --publication-venue "[Journal or conference]" --target-audience "[general|alumni|media]" --tone "[formal|friendly|inspiring]" --output "[output_file.json]"
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a fully customized press release with all metadata fields specified and output saved directly to a JSON file.

output_examples.input: Paper on quantum error correction from Tsinghua University researchers published in Nature, demonstrating 47 percent reduction in error rates through adaptive protocols.
output_examples.output: Generated press release with an engaging headline summarizing the breakthrough, dateline showing Tsinghua University and the current date, a lead paragraph highlighting the 47 percent improvement in accessible language, body sections explaining why quantum error correction matters for computing, a researcher quote about the discovery's significance, and institutional boilerplate for Tsinghua University.

output_examples.input: Technical abstract about a machine learning model achieving 94 percent accuracy in early cancer detection from a dataset of 50,000 medical images.
output_examples.output: Press release with an accessible headline about AI advancing cancer screening, simplified explanation of the machine learning approach written for general readers, the accuracy metric presented with appropriate medical context, a researcher comment on potential clinical impact, and background on why early detection improves patient outcomes.

output_examples.input: Minimal input with only paper text and no title, authors, or institution specified.
output_examples.output: Press release using default values where metadata is missing: headline derived from content analysis of the paper text, generic dateline, lead paragraph extracted from identified key findings, templated researcher quotes, and a generic institutional boilerplate. Assumptions made due to missing metadata are noted in the output.

best_practices: Always provide the full paper text or a detailed abstract for the most accurate and complete press release output
Specify the institution name and author list for accurate boilerplate text and properly attributed researcher quotes
Review and replace generated templated quotes with actual researcher comments before publishing the press release
anti_patterns: Do not publish generated press releases as official university communications without human editorial review and approval
Do not present templated researcher quotes as authentic statements from real researchers without verification and consent
Do not expect the tool to produce accurate results for papers in languages other than English or Chinese without testing

faq.question: What input does this skill need to generate a press release?
faq.answer: The skill requires the full paper text or a detailed abstract as the primary input. Optional inputs include the paper title, author names, institution name, publication venue, target audience, and desired tone.

faq.question: Can this skill generate press releases in languages other than English?
faq.answer: The skill is optimized for English and Chinese academic papers. The output language matches the input language. Support for other languages is limited and results may vary.

faq.question: How accurate are the generated researcher quotes?
faq.answer: The generated quotes are templated placeholders based on the author names provided. They are not actual statements from researchers. You should replace them with real quotes from the researchers before publishing.

faq.question: What output format does the skill produce?
faq.answer: The skill produces a structured JSON output containing headline, subheadline, dateline, lead paragraph, body paragraphs, researcher quotes, institutional boilerplate, and media contact information.

faq.question: Can I customize the tone and target audience of the press release?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill supports three tone styles (formal, friendly, inspiring) and three target audiences (general public, alumni, media). Adjust these parameters to match your communication goals.

faq.question: Does this skill require internet access or external APIs?
faq.answer: No, the skill runs entirely locally using only the Python standard library. It does not make any external API calls or require internet access to function.
